name: Stir Fried Fresh Lotus Root
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Dried tree ear mushrooms (torn into 1/2 inch pieces)
1/4 lb Snow peas (stems and strings removed)
recipeInstructions:
Use vegetable stock instead of chicken stock if you're adamant about avoiding animal products.
For cooking purposes, lotus roots can be treated as potatoes. The Japanese use them in tempura dishes and come up with an "exotic potato chip".
Cover dried black mushrooms and tree ears with water until soft and pliable (about 30 minutes). Rinse and squeeze out excess water from both mushrooms. Remove stems from black mushrooms; cut caps in half and set aside. Pinch off the hard center from tree ears; tear large pieces in half and set aside.
Rinse lotus root with cold water. Trim both ends of the bulb. Peel the skin. Cut bulbs lengthwise. Lay flat and cut crosswise into 1/8-inch-thick slices; set aside.
Plunge slices into acidulated water. Drain.
Over medium-high heat, preheat wok until hot. Add oil and tilt wok to coat sides. Add salt, ginger, and garlic; stir-fry until fragrant. Add onions; stir-fry for 15 seconds or until they begin to wilt. Add black mushrooms, lotus root, tree ears, water chestnuts, and snow peas; stir-fry for about 2 minutes or until snow peas turn bright green.
Sprinkle in the sugar, white pepper, and soy sauce; toss a few seconds.
Splash chicken stock against sides of hot wok and use to deglaze. Cover, reduce to medium heat; cook for 30 seconds. Remove cover, dribble in sesame oil. Serve hot.
Serves 8.
